description: Notion integration tokens, workspace sharing controls, and admin red flags. Covers both internal integrations (secret_…) and the newer ntn_ format used by Notion Connections / Workspace Integrations.

misconfigurations.title: Notion page set to 'Share to web' on a workspace containing internal data
misconfigurations.rationale: Public Notion pages are indexed by Google; multiple incident reports show PII / customer data leaking this way.
misconfigurations.fix: Workspace settings > Security > disable 'Share to web' at the workspace level and require admin approval for exceptions.

misconfigurations.title: Guests added without expiration policy
misconfigurations.rationale: Guest accounts persist forever by default; ex-vendor / ex-contractor access bleeds over.
misconfigurations.fix: Quarterly guest review + automation that removes guests with no activity > 90 days.

misconfigurations.title: Public Notion integration used where an internal one would suffice
misconfigurations.rationale: Public integrations can be installed by anyone with a workspace; widens blast radius.
misconfigurations.fix: Settings > Integrations > prefer 'Internal' integrations for org-owned automation.

misconfigurations.title: SAML SSO available (Enterprise plan) but not enforced
misconfigurations.rationale: Allowing both SSO and email-link login means token theft via personal email = workspace ATO.
misconfigurations.fix: Workspace settings > Identity & provisioning > enforce SSO, disable email-link login.

admin_red_flags.title: Workspace-wide export by a non-admin
admin_red_flags.rationale: `Export all workspace content` produces a complete data dump.
admin_red_flags.signal: Audit log: `workspace.exported` actor not in {Workspace Owner, Admin}.

admin_red_flags.title: Page newly shared to web from a database known to contain customer data
admin_red_flags.rationale: Catch the moment of mis-sharing before crawlers index.
admin_red_flags.signal: Audit log: `page.public_share_enabled` filtered to known-sensitive parent IDs.

admin_red_flags.title: Internal integration added to a top-level workspace page
admin_red_flags.rationale: Indicates someone wiring an API consumer; should be reviewed.
admin_red_flags.signal: Audit log: `integration.workspace_added` outside the IT-admin group.
